name: Scheduled Data Pipeline – ETL, Anomaly Detection & Alerting

nodes.name: Schedule – Every 6 Hours
nodes.notes: Runs every 6 hours. Can be changed to any interval via the cron expression.

parameters.query:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('hour', created_at) as hour,
  COUNT(*) as order_count,
  SUM(amount) as revenue,
  AVG(amount) as avg_order_value,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as unique_customers,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'failed' THEN 1 END) as failed_orders
FROM orders
WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('hour', created_at)
ORDER BY hour DESC;

nodes.name: Postgres – Fetch Last 7 Days Orders
nodes.notes: Aggregates hourly order metrics over the past 7 days. Replace with your actual DB schema.

nodes.name: Code – Z-Score Anomaly Detection
nodes.notes: Uses Z-score method (threshold=2.5σ) to detect revenue spikes, drops, and unusually high failure rates.

nodes.name: HTTP – LLM Diagnose Anomalies
nodes.notes: Generates a natural-language diagnosis of detected anomalies for the Slack alert.

parameters.query:
=INSERT INTO pipeline_runs (pipeline_run_id, ran_at, anomaly_count, total_revenue, avg_hourly_revenue, avg_hourly_orders, anomalies_json, status)
VALUES ('{{ $json.pipeline_run_id }}', '{{ $json.ran_at }}', {{ $json.anomaly_count }}, {{ $json.total_revenue }}, {{ $json.avg_hourly_revenue }}, {{ $json.avg_hourly_orders }}, '{{ $json.anomalies_json }}'::jsonb, '{{ $json.status }}');

nodes.name: Postgres – Log Pipeline Run
nodes.notes: Records every pipeline execution for observability, trend analysis, and audit trails.
